title: Chicken Tinga Tacos
steps: Season the chicken thighs with salt. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and brown the chicken on both sides, about 3 minutes per side (it won't be cooked through yet). Remove to a plate.
Add the remaining oil to the skillet and cook the sliced onion until soft and starting to brown, 5–6 minutes. Add the garlic and cook 30 seconds more.
Stir in the chipotles with their sauce, the diced tomatoes, broth, oregano, and cumin. Scrape up any browned bits from the pan.
Return the chicken to the skillet, cover, and simmer over medium-low for 15 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through.
Move the chicken to a cutting board and shred it with two forks. Stir it back into the sauce and simmer uncovered 5 minutes, until the sauce clings to the chicken. Taste for salt.
Warm the tortillas in a dry skillet or directly over a gas flame. Fill with the tinga and top with cilantro, cotija, and a squeeze of lime.
notes: Leftover chipotles freeze well in a zip bag — flatten it so you can snap off one pepper at a time.

title: Coconut Curry Noodle Soup
steps: Soak or cook the rice noodles according to the package, rinse with cool water, and set aside.
Heat the oil in a soup pot over medium heat. Add the curry paste, ginger, and garlic and fry for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Whisk in the coconut milk and let it bubble for 2 minutes, then add the broth, fish sauce, and brown sugar. Bring to a simmer.
Add the sliced chicken and bell pepper and simmer gently for 6–8 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through.
Take the pot off the heat and stir in the lime juice. Taste — it should be salty, sweet, and tangy; adjust with more fish sauce or lime.
Divide the noodles between bowls, ladle the soup over, and top generously with cilantro.
notes: Shrimp or tofu swap in easily for the chicken.

title: Quick Chana Masala
steps: Start the rice cooking however you usually do.
Heat the oil in a deep skillet over medium heat. Cook the onion with a pinch of salt until golden at the edges, 6–8 minutes.
Add the garlic, ginger, and tomato paste and cook 1 minute, stirring. Add the garam masala, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and cayenne and toast for 30 seconds.
Pour in the crushed tomatoes and ½ cup water. Simmer 5 minutes, until slightly thickened.
Add the chickpeas and simmer 10 minutes, mashing a few against the side of the pan to thicken the sauce. Add splashes of water if it gets too thick.
Off the heat, stir in the lemon juice and half the cilantro. Salt to taste. Serve over rice with the rest of the cilantro on top.
notes: Even better the next day — make a double batch.

title: Weeknight Bolognese
steps: Heat the olive oil in a wide pot over medium heat. Cook the onion, carrot, and celery with a big pinch of salt until soft, about 8 minutes.
Push the vegetables aside, raise the heat to medium-high, and add the beef. Break it up and cook until no longer pink, then keep going until some of it browns, about 8 minutes total.
Stir in the garlic and tomato paste and cook 2 minutes, until the paste darkens a shade.
Add the crushed tomatoes, milk, oregano, and bay leaf. Simmer gently, partly covered, for 30 minutes, stirring now and then. Add a splash of water if it gets thicker than you like.
Meanwhile cook the spaghetti in well-salted water to al dente; reserve a cup of pasta water before draining.
Fish out the bay leaf. Toss the pasta with the sauce and parmesan, loosening with pasta water as needed. Season and serve with more parmesan.
notes: The milk sounds odd but makes the sauce silky — don't skip it. Sauce freezes well.

title: Fluffy Buttermilk Pancakes

ingredients.prep: melted, plus more for the pan

steps: Whisk the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl.
In another bowl, whisk the buttermilk, eggs, melted butter, and vanilla.
Pour the wet into the dry and stir just until no dry streaks remain — lumps are fine. Let the batter rest 5 minutes.
Heat a griddle or nonstick skillet over medium heat and rub with a little butter.
Scoop ⅓-cup rounds and cook until bubbles pop on the surface and the edges look set, 2–3 minutes. Flip and cook 1–2 minutes more.
Keep warm in a 200°F oven while you finish the batch. Serve with butter and maple syrup.
notes: Extra pancakes freeze well — reheat in the toaster.

title: Buttermilk Oven-Fried Chicken
steps: Stir the buttermilk and hot sauce together in a bowl or zip bag, add the chicken, and marinate at least 30 minutes (overnight is better).
Heat the oven to 425°F. Set a wire rack on a foil-lined sheet pan and oil it lightly.
Mix the panko, parmesan, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper in a shallow dish, then drizzle in the melted butter and toss until the crumbs look like damp sand.
Lift each piece of chicken out of the buttermilk, let the excess drip off, and press firmly into the crumbs to coat all over. Set on the rack.
Bake 35–40 minutes, until deep golden and an instant-read thermometer hits 175°F at the bone.
Rest 5 minutes before serving.
notes: The leftover marinade buttermilk gets tossed, so this pairs nicely with pancakes the same week to finish the carton.
